topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence of the examination hall felt like a physical weight pressing down on my chest. When the professor’s voice cut through the quiet, pointing directly at my name, the world tilted. My hands, resting on the desk, began to tremble uncontrollably. I looked down at the paper in my lap, the carefully constructed sentences suddenly seeming flimsy, exposed. A coldness spread from my fingertips up my arms, making my skin prickle. Every eye in the room seemed to fix on me, and the sheer weight of the accusation settled in my stomach, a sickening, tight knot that made breathing shallow. I couldn't meet anyone’s gaze; I just stared at the ink, wishing the floor would swallow me whole.

[story 2]

"Are you prepared to address the allegations, Mr. Davies?" The words echoed in the sterile lecture room, each syllable sharp and unforgiving. I felt a sudden, dizzying rush of heat rising in my neck. My throat constricted, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I could feel the blood draining from my face, leaving a clammy sensation on my skin. My gaze darted around the room, landing on my classmates, and I saw only blank, unreadable expressions. A wave of nausea washed over me, so intense that I had to grip the edge of the table to steady myself. My mind raced, a frantic scramble to find an explanation that simply wasn't there.

[story 3]

The notification flashed across my screen, a stark, unwelcome beacon in the dim light of my dorm room. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drumbeat against the quiet hum of the ventilation system. I sat frozen in my chair, the laptop screen reflecting a pale, distorted image of my own face. When I finally managed to read the email—the formal charge—my vision blurred. My hands clenched into fists so tightly that my nails dug crescent moons into my palms. A strange detachment set in, as if I were watching the entire scene unfold from a great distance. I slowly pushed the chair back, the scrape of the legs on the floor sounding deafeningly loud in the sudden vacuum of my attention.
